edges.description: Functional lysosomal hydrolase deficiency leads to substrate accumulation.

edges.description: Connective-tissue and skeletal lysosomal storage produces progressive skeletal dysplasia.

edges.description: Joint and connective-tissue involvement restricts mobility.
edges.source: Lysosomal Substrate Accumulation in Connective Tissue

edges.description: Progressive skeletal and joint disease produces chronic pain and functional impairment.
edges.source: Lysosomal Substrate Accumulation in Connective Tissue

edges.description: Connective-tissue storage affects cardiac valves, producing valvular thickening and insufficiency.

nodes.description: Thickening and insufficiency of the mitral and aortic valves, usually mild.

evidence.snippet: thickening and insufficiency of the mitral and aortic valves

nodes.description: Carpal tunnel syndrome may require surgical release.

evidence.snippet:
carpal tunnel and, rarely, tarsal tunnel syndrome may
require surgica

nodes.description: Significant morbidity from chronic pain with severe functional impairment.

evidence.snippet:
severe functional impairment and significant morbidity from chronic
pain

meta.association: Biallelic GNPTG variants impairing the GlcNAc-1-phosphotransferase gamma subunit

evidence.snippet:
GNPTAB and GNPTG encode the α/β-precursor and the
γ-subunit of N-acetylglucosamine (GlcNAc)-1-phosphotransferase, respectively

nodes.description: Biallelic GNPTG variants impair the gamma subunit of GlcNAc-1-phosphotransferase, reducing mannose-6-phosphate tagging of soluble acid hydrolases. Untagged enzymes are missorted and hypersecreted into plasma rather than delivered to the lysosome, leaving the lysosome functionally deficient in multiple hydrolases.
nodes.id: GlcNAc-1-Phosphotransferase Gamma-Subunit Deficiency and M6P Targeting Failure

evidence.reference_title: Mucolipidosis type II and type III: a systematic review of 843 published cases.
evidence.snippet:
Mucolipidosis (ML) II, MLIII alpha/beta, and MLIII gamma are rare
autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disorders.

evidence.reference_title: The lysosomal storage disorders mucolipidosis type II, type III alpha/beta, and type III gamma: Update on GNPTAB and GNPTG mutations.
evidence.snippet:
MLIII alpha/beta and MLIII gamma are
attenuated forms of the disease

nodes.description: Progressive joint stiffness and restriction of movement.

evidence.snippet: mainly affecting skeletal, joint, and connective tissues

nodes.description: Functional deficiency of multiple lysosomal hydrolases leads to slowly progressive accumulation of undegraded substrates, predominantly affecting skeletal, joint, and connective tissues.
nodes.id: Lysosomal Substrate Accumulation in Connective Tissue

evidence.snippet: Mucolipidosis III gamma (ML IIIγ) is a slowly progressive inborn error of metabolism mainly affecting skeletal, joint, and connective tissues.

nodes.description: Progressive skeletal dysplasia (dysostosis) affecting the skeleton.

evidence.snippet: Mucolipidosis III gamma (ML IIIγ) is a slowly progressive inborn error of metabolism mainly affecting skeletal, joint, and connective tissues.
